name: IC Risk-Based Capital Framework (RBC2)
summary: Requires insurers to maintain capital adequacy based on risk exposure. Drives analytics investment for risk quantification, actuarial modeling, and stress testing.
requirements: Risk quantification across credit, market, insurance, and operational risk
Capital adequacy calculation with minimum RBC ratio of 100%
Stress testing under multiple adverse scenarios
Actuarial model governance with independent review
Quarterly RBC reporting to Insurance Commission
penalties: IC intervention escalation: enhanced monitoring, capital restoration plan, restriction on new business, conservatorship, and ultimately license revocation for persistent non-compliance.

sasCapabilityMapping.specificFeature: Integrated credit, market, and insurance risk quantification models
Automated RBC ratio calculation with IC-compliant reporting
Multi-scenario stress testing with reverse stress test capabilities
Model lifecycle management with validation workflows for actuarial models

competitivePositioning: SAS Risk Management is the only platform that combines risk quantification, stress testing, and model governance in one integrated solution. Competitors require stitching together spreadsheets, point solutions, and manual processes.
talkingPoints: Philippine insurers are moving from manual spreadsheet-based RBC to automated analytics
IC is increasing scrutiny on stress testing adequacy — manual approaches will not pass examination
SAS provides pre-built actuarial and risk models that accelerate RBC2 compliance
Model governance (SAS Model Manager) satisfies IC requirements for actuarial model review
Integrated platform reduces operational risk vs. spreadsheet-based calculations
